---
title: "PenicillinX-Project"
format: pdf
---

```{r OriginalChart, echo=FALSE, include=TRUE, message=FALSE, fig.cap="Cross reactivity matrix re-created from X et al"}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 10
#| out-width: "100%"
# Install if missing: BiocManager::install("ComplexHeatmap")
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)
# Make sure Gu are cited for the above libraries
# [@gu2014]
# [@gu2022]

data <- read.csv("~/PycharmProjects/PenicillinX-Project/Original.csv", row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)
mat <- as.matrix(data)

# Makes midline grey
diag(mat) <- -1

# Matches reference dataset
col_fn = colorRamp2(c(-1, 0, 1, 2, 3), c("#808080", "#A9D08F", "#FF0000","#CF734F","#FFFFFF"))


Heatmap(mat, 
        name = "Match",
        col = col_fn,
        
        # Labels
        row_names_side = "left",      # Moves antibiotic labels to the Left
        column_names_side = "top",    # Moves antibiotic labels to the Top
        
        # Grid
        rect_gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 0.4), # Adds the black grid lines
        cluster_rows = FALSE,         # Keep your specific list order
        cluster_columns = FALSE,
        
        
        # TEXT 
        column_names_rot = 90,        # Vertical text for top labels
        row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8),
        column_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8),
        
        show_heatmap_legend = FALSE,
        
# Matching the thick lines from reference dataset
        layer_fun = function(j, i, x, y, width, height, fill) {
          n_row = nrow(mat)
          n_col = ncol(mat)
          
          # Vertical lines 
          v_idx = seq(5, n_col - 1, by = 5)
          if(length(v_idx) > 0) {
            grid.segments(
              x0 = unit(v_idx / n_col, "npc"), 
              y0 = unit(0, "npc"),
              x1 = unit(v_idx / n_col, "npc"), 
              y1 = unit(1, "npc"),
              gp = gpar(lwd = 1.4, col = "black")
            )
          }
          
          # Horizontal lines 
          h_idx = seq(5, n_row - 1, by = 5)
          if(length(h_idx) > 0) {
            grid.segments(
              x0 = unit(0, "npc"), 
              y0 = unit(1 - (h_idx / n_row), "npc"),
              x1 = unit(1, "npc"), 
              y1 = unit(1 - (h_idx / n_row), "npc"),
              gp = gpar(lwd = 1.4, col = "black")
            )
          }
        }

)
```



```{python directMatchAlgorithm, echo=FALSE}
import pandas as pd
from rdkit import Chem

abxData = pd.read_csv("antibiotics_data.csv")
ABxList = abxData["Antibiotic"].tolist()

#len(set(abxData["SMILESR1"])) # Can delete, tells unique number of Side chains


mol_series = abxData.set_index("Antibiotic")["SMILESR1"].apply(
    lambda x: Chem.MolFromSmiles(x) if pd.notna(x) else None
)

names = ABxList
matrix_df = pd.DataFrame(0, index=names, columns=names)

for row_name in names:
    row_mol = mol_series[row_name]
    if row_mol is None:
        continue

    # Check both directions: A contains B OR B contains A
    matrix_df.loc[row_name] = mol_series.apply(
        lambda col_mol: 1 if (col_mol and row_mol and (
            row_mol.HasSubstructMatch(col_mol) or
            col_mol.HasSubstructMatch(row_mol)
        )) else 0
    )

matrix_df.to_csv('direct_match.csv', sep=',', index_label='Antibiotic')


```
<!-- Keep adjacent-->
```{r DirectMatchChart, echo=FALSE, include=TRUE, message=FALSE, fig.cap="Cross reactivity matrix using direct matching algorithm"}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 10
#| out-width: "100%"
# Install if missing: BiocManager::install("ComplexHeatmap")
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)
# Make sure Gu are cited for the above libraries
# [@gu2014]
# [@gu2022]

data <- read.csv("~/PycharmProjects/PenicillinX-Project/direct_match.csv", row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)
mat <- as.matrix(data)

# Makes midline grey
diag(mat) <- -1

# Matches reference dataset
col_fn = colorRamp2(c(-1, 0, 1), c("#808080", "#A9D08F", "#FF0000"))


Heatmap(mat, 
        name = "Match",
        col = col_fn,
        
        # Labels
        row_names_side = "left",      # Moves antibiotic labels to the Left
        column_names_side = "top",    # Moves antibiotic labels to the Top
        
        # Grid
        rect_gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 0.4), # Adds the black grid lines
        cluster_rows = FALSE,         # Keep your specific list order
        cluster_columns = FALSE,
        
        
        # TEXT 
        column_names_rot = 90,        # Vertical text for top labels
        row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8),
        column_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8),
        
        show_heatmap_legend = FALSE,
        
# Matching the thick lines from reference dataset
        layer_fun = function(j, i, x, y, width, height, fill) {
          n_row = nrow(mat)
          n_col = ncol(mat)
          
          # Vertical lines 
          v_idx = seq(5, n_col - 1, by = 5)
          if(length(v_idx) > 0) {
            grid.segments(
              x0 = unit(v_idx / n_col, "npc"), 
              y0 = unit(0, "npc"),
              x1 = unit(v_idx / n_col, "npc"), 
              y1 = unit(1, "npc"),
              gp = gpar(lwd = 1.4, col = "black")
            )
          }
          
          # Horizontal lines 
          h_idx = seq(5, n_row - 1, by = 5)
          if(length(h_idx) > 0) {
            grid.segments(
              x0 = unit(0, "npc"), 
              y0 = unit(1 - (h_idx / n_row), "npc"),
              x1 = unit(1, "npc"), 
              y1 = unit(1 - (h_idx / n_row), "npc"),
              gp = gpar(lwd = 1.4, col = "black")
            )
          }
        }

)
```


```{python RDKit_Dice_Threshold, echo=FALSE, include= TRUE}
from rdkit import DataStructs
from rdkit.Chem import rdFingerprintGenerator

similarity_threshold = 0.7
#print(f"Morgan Fingerprinting with Dice (Threshold: {similarity_threshold})")

# 1. Initialize the modern Fingerprint Generator (Radius 2 = ECFP4)
# fpSize=2048 is the standard default
fp_gen = rdFingerprintGenerator.GetMorganGenerator(radius=2, fpSize=2048)

# 2. Pre-convert SMILES to Mols
mol_series = abxData.set_index("Antibiotic")["SMILESR1"].apply(
    lambda x: Chem.MolFromSmiles(x) if pd.notna(x) else None
)

# 3. Pre-generate Count Fingerprints using the new generator
# GetCountFingerprint(m) creates the count-based vector needed for Dice
fp_series = mol_series.apply(
    lambda m: fp_gen.GetCountFingerprint(m) if m else None
)

names = ABxList
matrix_df = pd.DataFrame(0, index=names, columns=names)

for row_name in names:
    row_fp = fp_series[row_name]
    if row_fp is None:
        continue

    # 4. Calculate Dice Similarity with the threshold
    matrix_df.loc[row_name] = fp_series.apply(
        lambda col_fp: 1 if (col_fp and DataStructs.DiceSimilarity(row_fp, col_fp) >= similarity_threshold) else 0
    )

matrix_df.to_csv('MorganDice.csv', sep=',', index_label='Antibiotic')
```
<!-- Keep Adjacent-->
```{r MorganDiceChart, echo=FALSE, include=TRUE, message=FALSE, fig.cap="Cross reactivity matrix using Morgan Fingerprinting and Dice Similarity"}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 10
#| out-width: "100%"
# Install if missing: BiocManager::install("ComplexHeatmap")
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)
# Make sure Gu are cited for the above libraries
# [@gu2014]
# [@gu2022]

data <- read.csv("~/PycharmProjects/PenicillinX-Project/MorganDice.csv", row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)
mat <- as.matrix(data)

# Makes midline grey
diag(mat) <- -1

# Matches reference dataset
col_fn = colorRamp2(c(-1, 0, 1), c("#808080", "#A9D08F", "#FF0000"))


Heatmap(mat, 
        name = "Match",
        col = col_fn,
        
        # Labels
        row_names_side = "left",      # Moves antibiotic labels to the Left
        column_names_side = "top",    # Moves antibiotic labels to the Top
        
        # Grid
        rect_gp = gpar(col = "black", lwd = 0.4), # Adds the black grid lines
        cluster_rows = FALSE,         # Keep your specific list order
        cluster_columns = FALSE,
        
        
        # TEXT 
        column_names_rot = 90,        # Vertical text for top labels
        row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8),
        column_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8),
        
        show_heatmap_legend = FALSE,
        
# Matching the thick lines from reference dataset
        layer_fun = function(j, i, x, y, width, height, fill) {
          n_row = nrow(mat)
          n_col = ncol(mat)
          
          # Vertical lines 
          v_idx = seq(5, n_col - 1, by = 5)
          if(length(v_idx) > 0) {
            grid.segments(
              x0 = unit(v_idx / n_col, "npc"), 
              y0 = unit(0, "npc"),
              x1 = unit(v_idx / n_col, "npc"), 
              y1 = unit(1, "npc"),
              gp = gpar(lwd = 1.4, col = "black")
            )
          }
          
          # Horizontal lines 
          h_idx = seq(5, n_row - 1, by = 5)
          if(length(h_idx) > 0) {
            grid.segments(
              x0 = unit(0, "npc"), 
              y0 = unit(1 - (h_idx / n_row), "npc"),
              x1 = unit(1, "npc"), 
              y1 = unit(1 - (h_idx / n_row), "npc"),
              gp = gpar(lwd = 1.4, col = "black")
            )
          }
        }

)
```
